# Welcome {.unnumbered}

TODO: opening hook, two or three sentences, in the house register
(concrete, second person — see the reference course's "Most statistics
courses start with theory. This one starts with your data.").

This course teaches CDISC clinical data standards by building them: you
take raw data from a simulated Phase III trial (codename **GLPX-001**)
and carry it all the way to submission-ready datasets and outputs, in R.

::: callout-important
### Before You Start: Set Up Your Tools

Go to [**Before You Begin: Setup**](sessions/setup.qmd) and follow the
steps there. This course pins its package versions with `renv`; the
setup session explains why and gets you restored and rendering in a few
minutes. Nothing else in the course will work until you have done this.
:::

## Who This Course Is For {.unnumbered}

TODO: audience description — statistical programmers, biostatisticians,
data managers and researchers moving into clinical trial work in the
pharmaceutical industry.

::: callout-note
### Scope and level

TODO: what this course is and deliberately is not.
:::

## What This Course Expects From You {.unnumbered}

TODO: prerequisites (working R, comfort with the tidyverse, no prior
CDISC knowledge assumed).

## How the Course Is Organised {.unnumbered}

TODO: one short paragraph per part:

- **Part 1: Foundations** — why standards exist, and the GLPX-001 trial
  you will work with throughout.
- **Part 2: SDTM** — TODO.
- **Part 3: ADaM** — TODO.
- **Part 4: Outputs and Submission** — TODO.

All data in this course is **synthetic**, generated by
[`R/simulate_trial.R`](https://github.com/sufyansuleman/cdisc-with-r/blob/main/R/simulate_trial.R).
No real patient information is used anywhere.

> Exercises are included in this book; worked solutions, extra
> exercises and instructor material are in the solutions version (add link).
